subsection.title: Parametric curves
Velocity, speed, and acceleration
Arc length
subsection.p: For a curve \mathbf r(t), a\le t\le b, \boxed{L=\int_a^b |\mathbf r'(t)|\,dt.}

subsection.title: Slope of a parametric plane curve
subsection.p: If x'(t)\ne0, then \boxed{\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{dy/dt}{dx/dt}.} If x'(t)=0\quad\text{and}\quad y'(t)\ne0, the tangent is vertical.

subsection.title: Common parametrizations
Polar coordinates
subsection.p: A polar curve r=f(\theta) can be parametrized by \boxed{\mathbf r(\theta)=\bigl(f(\theta)\cos\theta,\ f(\theta)\sin\theta\bigr).}

subsection.title: Polar area
subsection.p: If r=f(\theta) traces the region once for \alpha\le\theta\le\beta, then \boxed{A=\frac12\int_\alpha^\beta f(\theta)^2\,d\theta.}

warning.title: Warning
warning.p: A parametrization remembers orientation and speed. The same geometric curve may give different line integrals if it is traced in the opposite direction or traced multiple times.
